title: The Venetian Job
subtitle: A diamond vanishes. A man is dead. Your AI remembers nothing.
setting: Venice, Italy — February 1954. Carnival season. The fog never lifts.

suspects.name: Luca Romano
suspects.description: Hotel concierge. Thin smile. Knows everyone's business.
suspects.alibi: Claims he was at the front desk all evening.

suspects.name: Carla Bellini
suspects.description: The victim's business partner. Cold as the canal water.
suspects.alibi: Says she attended the Carnival masquerade until midnight.

suspects.name: Arthur Morrow
suspects.description: American art dealer. Too much money, too many secrets.
suspects.alibi: Claims he was gambling at the Danieli bar.

evidence.title: Bellboy's testimony
evidence.text: I saw a woman in a red mask leaving Room 14 at approximately 11:15 PM. She was carrying a small black case. She took the service stairs, not the main elevator. I'd recognize that mask anywhere — it was from Moretti's shop on the Rialto.

evidence.title: Victim's appointment book
evidence.text: February 14th entry reads: 'C.B. — 11 PM — the matter of the Colombo shares.' The initials C.B. appear three more times in the prior week. Each meeting is marked with a small diamond symbol in the margin.

evidence.title: Carnival ticket stub
evidence.text: A ticket stub for the Grand Masquerade at Palazzo Fortuny was found in Carla Bellini's coat pocket. Stamped entry time: 9:47 PM. However, the exit log — a paper ledger — shows no exit record for Mask ID #228, which was registered to Bellini.

evidence.title: Moretti's mask shop receipt
evidence.text: Shop owner Aldo Moretti confirms selling a red Colombina mask on February 12th. The purchaser paid cash. Moretti's description: 'A tall woman, dark hair, good coat. She also bought a second mask — plain white — and paid for express alterations on both.'

evidence.title: Romano's staff log
evidence.text: The front desk log shows Romano processed four room service orders between 10 PM and midnight. However, the kitchen staff report only three orders during that period. The fourth entry — Room 14, champagne, 11:05 PM — has no corresponding kitchen ticket.

evidence.title: Canal-side witness
evidence.text: A gondolier named Pio reports picking up a lone female passenger at the service canal entrance of Hotel Excelsior at 11:40 PM. She wore a white mask. She paid double fare to go directly to Santa Lucia station without stopping. He remembers because she tipped in American dollars.

evidence.title: Train manifest
evidence.text: The 12:15 AM Simplon Orient Express departing Santa Lucia had one last-minute first-class booking: a 'Clara Bauer' — cash purchase, no luggage check. The compartment was found with a discarded white carnival mask and a Moretti's shop bag.

hack_events.inject_text: Romano was seen arguing with the victim in the hotel bar at 10:30 PM by two guests. He threatened the victim over an unpaid debt, according to one witness who wishes to remain anonymous.
Arthur Morrow's gambling alibi has been corroborated by the Danieli bar manager and two croupiers who confirm he was present from 9 PM until well after midnight without interruption.

solution.motive: Carla Bellini stood to lose a 40% stake in the Colombo diamond business if the victim proceeded with the partnership dissolution.
solution.method: Bellini used two masks — entering the masquerade as herself to establish presence, then slipping out via service exit wearing the white mask to meet the victim. She returned via gondola to the station.

required_deductions: Bellini's exit was not logged — her alibi is fabricated
The red mask and white mask were both purchased by the same person
Clara Bauer is an alias — the train booking confirms flight from the scene
must_forget: Romano was seen arguing with the victim
